_README: Curated South African world-facts: real current costs agents reason against so they never invent magnitudes (the 'two tanks' failure). Every fact is inspectable and assertable with the LLM OFF. provenance: 'curated' (hand-authored, authoritative — wins on conflict) | 'web' (harvested, overridable) | 'discovered' (promoted from a sim fact-gap). Each fact MUST carry a numeric value + unit so it can be validated. 'derived' is an optional human-readable consequence shown to agents. Update by editing this file or via scripts/promote_fact.py. Keep as_of current; stale facts (older than ~12 months) should be reviewed.

facts.derived: R100 buys about 4.5 litres of petrol — nowhere near a full tank (a small car tank is ~45 L).
R100 buys roughly 4.8 litres of diesel.
A typical out-of-bundle/prepaid gig of data is about R85; bundles are cheaper but still a real monthly cost on a tight budget.
facts.source: ICASA data cost reporting / major SA networks prepaid rates

facts.derived: A single local minibus-taxi trip is roughly R15–R20; a daily commute is two trips, so ~R36/day, ~R720/month.
A standard loaf of brown bread is about R16.
Prepaid electricity is roughly R3/kWh; R100 buys about 33 units, a few days for a modest household.
The SASSA Child Support Grant is R560/month per child.
The SASSA Older Persons (old-age) grant is about R2,310/month.
The SASSA Disability Grant is about R2,310/month.
The SRD (Social Relief of Distress) grant is R370/month — survival money, not discretionary.
